authors_text: Eric Zhang,Kai Wang,Xingqian Xu,Zhangyang Wang,Humphrey Shi
title: Forget-Me-Not: Learning to Forget in Text-to-Image Diffusion Models
title_zh: 勿忘我：在文本到图像扩散模型中学习遗忘
abstract: The unlearning problem of deep learning models, once primarily an academic concern, has become a prevalent issue in the industry. The significant advances in text-to-image generation techniques have prompted global discussions on privacy, copyright, and safety, as numerous unauthorized personal IDs, content, artistic creations, and potentially harmful materials have been learned by these models and later utilized to generate and distribute uncontrolled content. To address this challenge, we propose \textbf{Forget-Me-Not}, an efficient and low-cost solution designed to safely remove specified IDs, objects, or styles from a well-configured text-to-image model in as little as 30 seconds, without impairing its ability to generate other content. Alongside our method, we introduce the \textbf{Memorization Score (M-Score)} and \textbf{ConceptBench} to measure the models' capacity to generate general concepts, grouped into three primary categories: ID, object, and style. Using M-Score and ConceptBench, we demonstrate that Forget-Me-Not can effectively eliminate targeted concepts while maintaining the model's performance on other concepts. Furthermore, Forget-Me-Not offers two practical extensions: a) removal of potentially harmful or NSFW content, and b) enhancement of model accuracy, inclusion and diversity through \textbf{concept correction and disentanglement}. It can also be adapted as a lightweight model patch for Stable Diffusion, allowing for concept manipulation and convenient distribution. To encourage future research in this critical area and promote the development of safe and inclusive generative models, we will open-source our code and ConceptBench at \href{https://github.com/SHI-Labs/Forget-Me-Not}{https://github.com/SHI-Labs/Forget-Me-Not}.
summary: The unlearning problem of deep learning models, once primarily an academic concern, has become a prevalent issue in the industry. The significant advances in text-to-image generation techniques have prompted global discussions on privacy, copyright, and safety, as numerous unauthorized personal IDs, content, artistic creations, and potentially harmful materials have been learned by these models and later utilized to generate and distribute uncontrolled content. To address this challenge, we propose \textbf{Forget-Me-Not}, an efficient and low-cost solution designed to safely remove specified IDs, objects, or styles from a well-configured text-to-image model in as little as 30 seconds, without impairing its ability to generate other content. Alongside our method, we introduce the \textbf{Memorization Score (M-Score)} and \textbf{ConceptBench} to measure the models' capacity to generate general concepts, grouped into three primary categories: ID, object, and style. Using M-Score and ConceptBench, we demonstrate that Forget-Me-Not can effectively eliminate targeted concepts while maintaining the model's performance on other concepts. Furthermore, Forget-Me-Not offers two practical extensions: a) removal of potentially harmful or NSFW content, and b) enhancement of model accuracy, inclusion and diversity through \textbf{concept correction and disentanglement}. It can also be adapted as a lightweight model patch for Stable Diffusion, allowing for concept manipulation and convenient distribution. To encourage future research in this critical area and promote the development of safe and inclusive generative models, we will open-source our code and ConceptBench at \href{https://github.com/SHI-Labs/Forget-Me-Not}{https://github.com/SHI-Labs/Forget-Me-Not}.
abstract_zh: 深度学习模型的遗忘问题曾经主要是学术界关注的问题，现已成为业界普遍存在的问题。文本到图像生成技术的重大进步引发了关于隐私、版权和安全的全球讨论，因为这些模型已学习了大量未经授权的个人 ID、内容、艺术创作和潜在有害材料，并随后用于生成和分发不受控制的内容。为了应对这一挑战，我们提出了 \textbf{Forget-Me-Not}，这是一种高效且低成本的解决方案，旨在在短短 30 秒内从配置良好的文本到图像模型中安全地删除指定的 ID、对象或样式，而不会削弱其生成其他内容的能力。除了我们的方法之外，我们还引入了 \textbf{Memorization Score (M-Score)} 和 \textbf{ConceptBench} 来衡量模型生成一般概念的能力，这些概念分为三个主要类别：ID、对象和风格。使用 M-Score 和 ConceptBench，我们证明勿忘我可以有效消除目标概念，同时保持模型在其他概念上的性能。此外，勿忘我提供了两个实用的扩展：a）删除潜在有害或 NSFW 内容，b）通过 \textbf{概念校正和解开}增强模型的准确性、包容性和多样性。它还可以改编为稳定扩散的轻量级模型补丁，允许概念操作和方便的分发。为了鼓励这一关键领域的未来研究并促进安全和包容性生成模型的开发，我们将在 \href{https://github.com/SHI-Labs/Forget-Me-Not}{https://github.com/SHI-Labs/Forget-Me-Not} 开源我们的代码和 ConceptBench。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种名为Forget-Me-Not的方法，用于在文本到图像扩散模型中高效安全地删除指定的ID、对象或风格，同时保持模型生成其他内容的能力。
AI.motivation: 随着文本到图像生成技术的普及，模型可能学习到未经授权的个人身份、版权内容或有害信息，引发隐私、版权和安全问题，亟需一种高效的方法来移除特定概念。
AI.method: 提出Forget-Me-Not方法，结合Memorization Score（M-Score）和ConceptBench评估体系，通过快速微调（30秒内）实现目标概念的删除，并提供有害内容移除和概念修正等扩展功能。
AI.result: 实验表明Forget-Me-Not能有效消除目标概念且不影响其他内容生成，支持Stable Diffusion的轻量级插件形式，并通过开源代码和ConceptBench推动安全生成模型研究。
AI.conclusion: 该研究为生成模型的隐私保护与内容安全提供了可行方案，通过开源促进学术界对模型遗忘能力的进一步探索。

search_text: Forget-Me-Not: Learning to Forget in Text-to-Image Diffusion Models 勿忘我：在文本到图像扩散模型中学习遗忘 The unlearning problem of deep learning models, once primarily an academic concern, has become a prevalent issue in the industry. The significant advances in text-to-image generation techniques have prompted global discussions on privacy, copyright, and safety, as numerous unauthorized personal IDs, content, artistic creations, and potentially harmful materials have been learned by these models and later utilized to generate and distribute uncontrolled content. To address this challenge, we propose \textbf{Forget-Me-Not}, an efficient and low-cost solution designed to safely remove specified IDs, objects, or styles from a well-configured text-to-image model in as little as 30 seconds, without impairing its ability to generate other content. Alongside our method, we introduce the \textbf{Memorization Score (M-Score)} and \textbf{ConceptBench} to measure the models' capacity to generate general concepts, grouped into three primary categories: ID, object, and style. Using M-Score and ConceptBench, we demonstrate that Forget-Me-Not can effectively eliminate targeted concepts while maintaining the model's performance on other concepts. Furthermore, Forget-Me-Not offers two practical extensions: a) removal of potentially harmful or NSFW content, and b) enhancement of model accuracy, inclusion and diversity through \textbf{concept correction and disentanglement}. It can also be adapted as a lightweight model patch for Stable Diffusion, allowing for concept manipulation and convenient distribution. To encourage future research in this critical area and promote the development of safe and inclusive generative models, we will open-source our code and ConceptBench at \href{https://github.com/SHI-Labs/Forget-Me-Not}{https://github.com/SHI-Labs/Forget-Me-Not}. 深度学习模型的遗忘问题曾经主要是学术界关注的问题，现已成为业界普遍存在的问题。文本到图像生成技术的重大进步引发了关于隐私、版权和安全的全球讨论，因为这些模型已学习了大量未经授权的个人 ID、内容、艺术创作和潜在有害材料，并随后用于生成和分发不受控制的内容。为了应对这一挑战，我们提出了 \textbf{Forget-Me-Not}，这是一种高效且低成本的解决方案，旨在在短短 30 秒内从配置良好的文本到图像模型中安全地删除指定的 ID、对象或样式，而不会削弱其生成其他内容的能力。除了我们的方法之外，我们还引入了 \textbf{Memorization Score (M-Score)} 和 \textbf{ConceptBench} 来衡量模型生成一般概念的能力，这些概念分为三个主要类别：ID、对象和风格。使用 M-Score 和 ConceptBench，我们证明勿忘我可以有效消除目标概念，同时保持模型在其他概念上的性能。此外，勿忘我提供了两个实用的扩展：a）删除潜在有害或 NSFW 内容，b）通过 \textbf{概念校正和解开}增强模型的准确性、包容性和多样性。它还可以改编为稳定扩散的轻量级模型补丁，允许概念操作和方便的分发。为了鼓励这一关键领域的未来研究并促进安全和包容性生成模型的开发，我们将在 \href{https://github.com/SHI-Labs/Forget-Me-Not}{https://github.com/SHI-Labs/Forget-Me-Not} 开源我们的代码和 ConceptBench。 本文提出了一种名为Forget-Me-Not的方法，用于在文本到图像扩散模型中高效安全地删除指定的ID、对象或风格，同时保持模型生成其他内容的能力。 Eric Zhang,Kai Wang,Xingqian Xu,Zhangyang Wang,Humphrey Shi cs.CV,cs.AI,cs.LG

authors_text: Paola Cascante-Bonilla,Khaled Shehada,James Seale Smith,Sivan Doveh,Donghyun Kim,Rameswar Panda,Gül Varol,Aude Oliva,Vicente Ordonez,Rogerio Feris,Leonid Karlinsky
title: Going Beyond Nouns With Vision & Language Models Using Synthetic Data
title_zh: 使用合成数据的视觉和语言模型超越名词
comment: Accepted to ICCV 2023. Project page: https://synthetic-vic.github.io/
comments: Accepted to ICCV 2023. Project page: https://synthetic-vic.github.io/
abstract: Large-scale pre-trained Vision & Language (VL) models have shown remarkable performance in many applications, enabling replacing a fixed set of supported classes with zero-shot open vocabulary reasoning over (almost arbitrary) natural language prompts. However, recent works have uncovered a fundamental weakness of these models. For example, their difficulty to understand Visual Language Concepts (VLC) that go 'beyond nouns' such as the meaning of non-object words (e.g., attributes, actions, relations, states, etc.), or difficulty in performing compositional reasoning such as understanding the significance of the order of the words in a sentence. In this work, we investigate to which extent purely synthetic data could be leveraged to teach these models to overcome such shortcomings without compromising their zero-shot capabilities. We contribute Synthetic Visual Concepts (SyViC) - a million-scale synthetic dataset and data generation codebase allowing to generate additional suitable data to improve VLC understanding and compositional reasoning of VL models. Additionally, we propose a general VL finetuning strategy for effectively leveraging SyViC towards achieving these improvements. Our extensive experiments and ablations on VL-Checklist, Winoground, and ARO benchmarks demonstrate that it is possible to adapt strong pre-trained VL models with synthetic data significantly enhancing their VLC understanding (e.g. by 9.9% on ARO and 4.3% on VL-Checklist) with under 1% drop in their zero-shot accuracy.
summary: Large-scale pre-trained Vision & Language (VL) models have shown remarkable performance in many applications, enabling replacing a fixed set of supported classes with zero-shot open vocabulary reasoning over (almost arbitrary) natural language prompts. However, recent works have uncovered a fundamental weakness of these models. For example, their difficulty to understand Visual Language Concepts (VLC) that go 'beyond nouns' such as the meaning of non-object words (e.g., attributes, actions, relations, states, etc.), or difficulty in performing compositional reasoning such as understanding the significance of the order of the words in a sentence. In this work, we investigate to which extent purely synthetic data could be leveraged to teach these models to overcome such shortcomings without compromising their zero-shot capabilities. We contribute Synthetic Visual Concepts (SyViC) - a million-scale synthetic dataset and data generation codebase allowing to generate additional suitable data to improve VLC understanding and compositional reasoning of VL models. Additionally, we propose a general VL finetuning strategy for effectively leveraging SyViC towards achieving these improvements. Our extensive experiments and ablations on VL-Checklist, Winoground, and ARO benchmarks demonstrate that it is possible to adapt strong pre-trained VL models with synthetic data significantly enhancing their VLC understanding (e.g. by 9.9% on ARO and 4.3% on VL-Checklist) with under 1% drop in their zero-shot accuracy.
abstract_zh: 大规模预训练视觉和语言 (VL) 模型在许多应用中表现出了卓越的性能，能够用针对（几乎任意）自然语言提示的零样本开放词汇推理来替换一组固定的受支持类。然而，最近的研究发现了这些模型的根本弱点。例如，他们难以理解“超越名词”的视觉语言概念（VLC），例如非宾语词的含义（例如属性、动作、关系、状态等），或者难以进行组合推理，例如理解句子中单词顺序的重要性。在这项工作中，我们研究了在多大程度上可以利用纯合成数据来教导这些模型克服这些缺点，而不损害其零样本能力。我们贡献了 Synthetic Visual Concepts (SyViC)——一个百万规模的合成数据集和数据生成代码库，允许生成额外的合适数据以提高 VLC 理解和 VL 模型的组合推理。此外，我们提出了一种通用的 VL 微调策略，以有效利用 SyViC 来实现这些改进。我们对 VL-Checklist、Winoground 和 ARO 基准进行的广泛实验和消融表明，可以使用合成数据来调整强大的预训练 VL 模型，从而显着增强其 VLC 理解（例如，ARO 提高 9.9%，VL-Checklist 提高 4.3%），而零样本准确率下降不到 1%。

AI.tldr: 本文提出利用合成数据提升视觉与语言模型对超越名词的视觉语言概念（VLC）及组合推理能力的理解。
AI.motivation: 现有大规模预训练视觉语言模型在处理非对象词（如属性、动作、关系等）及组合推理任务时表现不足，限制了其零样本推理能力的全面性。
AI.method: 构建百万级合成数据集SyViC及生成代码库，并设计通用微调策略，通过合成数据增强模型对VLC和组合推理的学习。
AI.result: 在VL-Checklist、Winoground和ARO基准测试中，模型VLC理解能力显著提升（如ARO提升9.9%，VL-Checklist提升4.3%），零样本准确率仅下降不足1%。
AI.conclusion: 合成数据能有效弥补视觉语言模型在非对象词理解及组合推理上的短板，且不影响其零样本能力。

authors_text: Qingyang Wang,Michael A. Powell,Ali Geisa,Eric W. Bridgeford,Joshua T. Vogelstein
title: Polarity is all you need to learn and transfer faster
title_zh: 极性是您更快学习和迁移所需的一切
abstract: Natural intelligences (NIs) thrive in a dynamic world - they learn quickly, sometimes with only a few samples. In contrast, artificial intelligences (AIs) typically learn with a prohibitive number of training samples and computational power. What design principle difference between NI and AI could contribute to such a discrepancy? Here, we investigate the role of weight polarity: development processes initialize NIs with advantageous polarity configurations; as NIs grow and learn, synapse magnitudes update, yet polarities are largely kept unchanged. We demonstrate with simulation and image classification tasks that if weight polarities are adequately set a priori, then networks learn with less time and data. We also explicitly illustrate situations in which a priori setting the weight polarities is disadvantageous for networks. Our work illustrates the value of weight polarities from the perspective of statistical and computational efficiency during learning.
summary: Natural intelligences (NIs) thrive in a dynamic world - they learn quickly, sometimes with only a few samples. In contrast, artificial intelligences (AIs) typically learn with a prohibitive number of training samples and computational power. What design principle difference between NI and AI could contribute to such a discrepancy? Here, we investigate the role of weight polarity: development processes initialize NIs with advantageous polarity configurations; as NIs grow and learn, synapse magnitudes update, yet polarities are largely kept unchanged. We demonstrate with simulation and image classification tasks that if weight polarities are adequately set a priori, then networks learn with less time and data. We also explicitly illustrate situations in which a priori setting the weight polarities is disadvantageous for networks. Our work illustrates the value of weight polarities from the perspective of statistical and computational efficiency during learning.
abstract_zh: 自然智能 (NI) 在动态世界中蓬勃发展 - 它们学习速度很快，有时只需要几个样本。相比之下，人工智能 (AI) 通常需要大量的训练样本和计算能力来进行学习。 NI 和 AI 之间的哪些设计原则差异可能导致这种差异？在这里，我们研究了权重极性的作用：开发过程用有利的极性配置初始化 NI；随着 NI 的成长和学习，突触幅度会更新，但极性基本上保持不变。我们通过模拟和图像分类任务证明，如果先验地充分设置权重极性，那么网络可以用更少的时间和数据进行学习。我们还明确说明了先验设置权重极性对网络不利的情况。我们的工作从学习过程中统计和计算效率的角度说明了权重极性的价值。

AI.tldr: 研究权重极性对神经网络学习效率的影响，探索其在减少训练时间和数据需求中的作用。
AI.motivation: 解决AI需要大量数据和计算资源的问题，借鉴自然智能的极性配置优势以提升学习效率。
AI.method: 通过模拟实验和图像分类任务，分析预先设定权重极性对网络学习效率的影响。
AI.result: 实验表明，合理预设权重极性可提升学习效率，但在特定情况下可能不利。
AI.conclusion: 权重极性对统计和计算效率有重要影响，合理设置可优化学习过程，但需根据任务调整。

search_text: Polarity is all you need to learn and transfer faster 极性是您更快学习和迁移所需的一切 Natural intelligences (NIs) thrive in a dynamic world - they learn quickly, sometimes with only a few samples. In contrast, artificial intelligences (AIs) typically learn with a prohibitive number of training samples and computational power. What design principle difference between NI and AI could contribute to such a discrepancy? Here, we investigate the role of weight polarity: development processes initialize NIs with advantageous polarity configurations; as NIs grow and learn, synapse magnitudes update, yet polarities are largely kept unchanged. We demonstrate with simulation and image classification tasks that if weight polarities are adequately set a priori, then networks learn with less time and data. We also explicitly illustrate situations in which a priori setting the weight polarities is disadvantageous for networks. Our work illustrates the value of weight polarities from the perspective of statistical and computational efficiency during learning. 自然智能 (NI) 在动态世界中蓬勃发展 - 它们学习速度很快，有时只需要几个样本。相比之下，人工智能 (AI) 通常需要大量的训练样本和计算能力来进行学习。 NI 和 AI 之间的哪些设计原则差异可能导致这种差异？在这里，我们研究了权重极性的作用：开发过程用有利的极性配置初始化 NI；随着 NI 的成长和学习，突触幅度会更新，但极性基本上保持不变。我们通过模拟和图像分类任务证明，如果先验地充分设置权重极性，那么网络可以用更少的时间和数据进行学习。我们还明确说明了先验设置权重极性对网络不利的情况。我们的工作从学习过程中统计和计算效率的角度说明了权重极性的价值。 研究权重极性对神经网络学习效率的影响，探索其在减少训练时间和数据需求中的作用。 Qingyang Wang,Michael A. Powell,Ali Geisa,Eric W. Bridgeford,Joshua T. Vogelstein cs.LG,cs.CV,cs.NE,q-bio.NC

authors_text: Sachin Shah,Sakshum Kulshrestha,Christopher A. Metzler
title: TiDy-PSFs: Computational Imaging with Time-Averaged Dynamic Point-Spread-Functions
title_zh: TiDy-PSF：具有时间平均动态点扩散函数的计算成像
abstract: Point-spread-function (PSF) engineering is a powerful computational imaging techniques wherein a custom phase mask is integrated into an optical system to encode additional information into captured images. Used in combination with deep learning, such systems now offer state-of-the-art performance at monocular depth estimation, extended depth-of-field imaging, lensless imaging, and other tasks. Inspired by recent advances in spatial light modulator (SLM) technology, this paper answers a natural question: Can one encode additional information and achieve superior performance by changing a phase mask dynamically over time? We first prove that the set of PSFs described by static phase masks is non-convex and that, as a result, time-averaged PSFs generated by dynamic phase masks are fundamentally more expressive. We then demonstrate, in simulation, that time-averaged dynamic (TiDy) phase masks can offer substantially improved monocular depth estimation and extended depth-of-field imaging performance.
summary: Point-spread-function (PSF) engineering is a powerful computational imaging techniques wherein a custom phase mask is integrated into an optical system to encode additional information into captured images. Used in combination with deep learning, such systems now offer state-of-the-art performance at monocular depth estimation, extended depth-of-field imaging, lensless imaging, and other tasks. Inspired by recent advances in spatial light modulator (SLM) technology, this paper answers a natural question: Can one encode additional information and achieve superior performance by changing a phase mask dynamically over time? We first prove that the set of PSFs described by static phase masks is non-convex and that, as a result, time-averaged PSFs generated by dynamic phase masks are fundamentally more expressive. We then demonstrate, in simulation, that time-averaged dynamic (TiDy) phase masks can offer substantially improved monocular depth estimation and extended depth-of-field imaging performance.
abstract_zh: 点扩散函数 (PSF) 工程是一种强大的计算成像技术，其中将定制相位掩模集成到光学系统中，以将附加信息编码到捕获的图像中。与深度学习结合使用，此类系统现在在单目深度估计、扩展景深成像、无透镜成像和其他任务方面提供最先进的性能。受空间光调制器 (SLM) 技术最新进展的启发，本文回答了一个自然问题：能否通过随时间动态改变相位掩模来编码附加信息并实现卓越性能？我们首先证明静态相位掩模描述的 PSF 集合是非凸的，因此，动态相位掩模生成的时间平均 PSF 从根本上更具表现力。然后，我们在仿真中证明，时间平均动态 (TiDy) 相位掩模可以显着改进单目深度估计和扩展景深成像性能。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于时间平均动态点扩散函数（TiDy-PSFs）的计算成像方法，通过动态调整相位掩模提升图像信息编码能力。
AI.motivation: 静态相位掩模的PSF集合存在非凸性限制，动态调整相位掩模可增强信息编码能力并突破性能瓶颈。
AI.method: 理论证明静态PSF的非凸性，设计动态相位掩模并通过仿真验证其时间平均PSF的性能优势。
AI.result: 仿真表明TiDy-PSFs在单目深度估计和扩展景深成像任务中较静态方法性能显著提升。
AI.conclusion: 动态相位掩模的时间平均PSF具有更强表达性，为计算成像提供了新思路和优化方向。

authors_text: Ahmad Amine,Mostafa Aldilati,Hadi Hasan,Noel Maalouf,Imad H. Elhajj
title: Human-Robot Interaction using VAHR: Virtual Assistant, Human, and Robots in the Loop
title_zh: 使用 VAHR 进行人机交互：循环中的虚拟助理、人类和机器人
abstract: Robots have become ubiquitous tools in various industries and households, highlighting the importance of human-robot interaction (HRI). This has increased the need for easy and accessible communication between humans and robots. Recent research has focused on the intersection of virtual assistant technology, such as Amazon's Alexa, with robots and its effect on HRI. This paper presents the Virtual Assistant, Human, and Robots in the loop (VAHR) system, which utilizes bidirectional communication to control multiple robots through Alexa. VAHR's performance was evaluated through a human-subjects experiment, comparing objective and subjective metrics of traditional keyboard and mouse interfaces to VAHR. The results showed that VAHR required 41% less Robot Attention Demand and ensured 91% more Fan-out time compared to the standard method. Additionally, VAHR led to a 62.5% improvement in multi-tasking, highlighting the potential for efficient human-robot interaction in physically- and mentally-demanding scenarios. However, subjective metrics revealed a need for human operators to build confidence and trust with this new method of operation.
summary: Robots have become ubiquitous tools in various industries and households, highlighting the importance of human-robot interaction (HRI). This has increased the need for easy and accessible communication between humans and robots. Recent research has focused on the intersection of virtual assistant technology, such as Amazon's Alexa, with robots and its effect on HRI. This paper presents the Virtual Assistant, Human, and Robots in the loop (VAHR) system, which utilizes bidirectional communication to control multiple robots through Alexa. VAHR's performance was evaluated through a human-subjects experiment, comparing objective and subjective metrics of traditional keyboard and mouse interfaces to VAHR. The results showed that VAHR required 41% less Robot Attention Demand and ensured 91% more Fan-out time compared to the standard method. Additionally, VAHR led to a 62.5% improvement in multi-tasking, highlighting the potential for efficient human-robot interaction in physically- and mentally-demanding scenarios. However, subjective metrics revealed a need for human operators to build confidence and trust with this new method of operation.
abstract_zh: 机器人已成为各行业和家庭中无处不在的工具，凸显了人机交互（HRI）的重要性。这增加了人类和机器人之间轻松、可访问的通信的需求。最近的研究重点是虚拟助理技术（例如亚马逊的 Alexa）与机器人的交叉及其对 HRI 的影响。本文介绍了虚拟助理、人类和机器人在环 (VAHR) 系统，该系统利用双向通信通过 Alexa 控制多个机器人。 VAHR 的性能通过人体实验进行评估，将传统键盘和鼠标界面的客观和主观指标与 VAHR 进行比较。结果表明，与标准方法相比，VAHR 所需的机器人注意力需求减少了 41%，并确保扇出时间延长了 91%。此外，VAHR 使多任务处理能力提高了 62.5%，凸显了在身体和精神要求较高的场景中高效人机交互的潜力。然而，主观指标表明，人类操作员需要通过这种新的操作方法建立信心和信任。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种名为VAHR的系统，通过虚拟助手（如Alexa）实现人类、虚拟助手与机器人之间的双向通信，以提升人机交互效率。
AI.motivation: 随着机器人在各领域的普及，传统的人机交互方式（如键盘鼠标）存在效率低、操作复杂等问题，亟需更高效且易用的交互方法。
AI.method: 设计VAHR系统，利用Alexa实现多机器人控制，并通过对比实验将VAHR与传统键盘鼠标界面在客观和主观指标上进行性能评估。
AI.result: VAHR系统使机器人注意力需求降低41%，任务执行时间延长91%，多任务处理效率提升62.5%，但用户对新交互方式的信任度仍需提升。
AI.conclusion: VAHR系统在提升人机交互效率方面表现显著，但需进一步优化用户对虚拟助手控制方式的信任感以实现广泛应用。

However, subjective metrics revealed a need for human operators to build confidence and trust with this new method of operation. 机器人已成为各行业和家庭中无处不在的工具，凸显了人机交互（HRI）的重要性。这增加了人类和机器人之间轻松、可访问的通信的需求。最近的研究重点是虚拟助理技术（例如亚马逊的 Alexa）与机器人的交叉及其对 HRI 的影响。本文介绍了虚拟助理、人类和机器人在环 (VAHR) 系统，该系统利用双向通信通过 Alexa 控制多个机器人。 VAHR 的性能通过人体实验进行评估，将传统键盘和鼠标界面的客观和主观指标与 VAHR 进行比较。结果表明，与标准方法相比，VAHR 所需的机器人注意力需求减少了 41%，并确保扇出时间延长了 91%。此外，VAHR 使多任务处理能力提高了 62.5%，凸显了在身体和精神要求较高的场景中高效人机交互的潜力。然而，主观指标表明，人类操作员需要通过这种新的操作方法建立信心和信任。 本文提出了一种名为VAHR的系统，通过虚拟助手（如Alexa）实现人类、虚拟助手与机器人之间的双向通信，以提升人机交互效率。 Ahmad Amine,Mostafa Aldilati,Hadi Hasan,Noel Maalouf,Imad H. Elhajj cs.RO

authors_text: Yongliang Shen,Kaitao Song,Xu Tan,Dongsheng Li,Weiming Lu,Yueting Zhuang
title: HuggingGPT: Solving AI Tasks with ChatGPT and its Friends in Hugging Face
title_zh: HuggingGPT：使用 ChatGPT 及其 Hugging Face 朋友解决 AI 任务
abstract: Solving complicated AI tasks with different domains and modalities is a key step toward artificial general intelligence. While there are numerous AI models available for various domains and modalities, they cannot handle complicated AI tasks autonomously. Considering large language models (LLMs) have exhibited exceptional abilities in language understanding, generation, interaction, and reasoning, we advocate that LLMs could act as a controller to manage existing AI models to solve complicated AI tasks, with language serving as a generic interface to empower this. Based on this philosophy, we present HuggingGPT, an LLM-powered agent that leverages LLMs (e.g., ChatGPT) to connect various AI models in machine learning communities (e.g., Hugging Face) to solve AI tasks. Specifically, we use ChatGPT to conduct task planning when receiving a user request, select models according to their function descriptions available in Hugging Face, execute each subtask with the selected AI model, and summarize the response according to the execution results. By leveraging the strong language capability of ChatGPT and abundant AI models in Hugging Face, HuggingGPT can tackle a wide range of sophisticated AI tasks spanning different modalities and domains and achieve impressive results in language, vision, speech, and other challenging tasks, which paves a new way towards the realization of artificial general intelligence.
summary: Solving complicated AI tasks with different domains and modalities is a key step toward artificial general intelligence. While there are numerous AI models available for various domains and modalities, they cannot handle complicated AI tasks autonomously. Considering large language models (LLMs) have exhibited exceptional abilities in language understanding, generation, interaction, and reasoning, we advocate that LLMs could act as a controller to manage existing AI models to solve complicated AI tasks, with language serving as a generic interface to empower this. Based on this philosophy, we present HuggingGPT, an LLM-powered agent that leverages LLMs (e.g., ChatGPT) to connect various AI models in machine learning communities (e.g., Hugging Face) to solve AI tasks. Specifically, we use ChatGPT to conduct task planning when receiving a user request, select models according to their function descriptions available in Hugging Face, execute each subtask with the selected AI model, and summarize the response according to the execution results. By leveraging the strong language capability of ChatGPT and abundant AI models in Hugging Face, HuggingGPT can tackle a wide range of sophisticated AI tasks spanning different modalities and domains and achieve impressive results in language, vision, speech, and other challenging tasks, which paves a new way towards the realization of artificial general intelligence.
abstract_zh: 解决具有不同领域和模式的复杂人工智能任务是迈向通用人工智能的关键一步。尽管有许多适用于不同领域和模式的人工智能模型，但它们无法自主处理复杂的人工智能任务。考虑到大型语言模型（LLM）在语言理解、生成、交互和推理方面表现出卓越的能力，我们主张LLM可以作为控制器来管理现有的AI模型以解决复杂的AI任务，而语言作为通用接口来实现这一点。基于这一理念，我们推出了 HuggingGPT，这是一种由 LLM 驱动的代理，它利用 LLM（例如 ChatGPT）连接机器学习社区中的各种 AI 模型（例如 Hugging Face）来解决 AI 任务。具体来说，我们在收到用户请求时使用 ChatGPT 进行任务规划，根据 Hugging Face 中提供的功能描述选择模型，使用所选的 AI 模型执行每个子任务，并根据执行结果汇总响应。通过利用ChatGPT强大的语言能力和Hugging Face中丰富的人工智能模型，HuggingGPT可以解决跨模态和领域的广泛复杂的人工智能任务，并在语言、视觉、语音和其他具有挑战性的任务中取得令人印象深刻的结果，为通用人工智能的实现开辟了新的途径。

AI.tldr: 本文提出HuggingGPT，利用ChatGPT等大语言模型作为控制器，通过Hugging Face平台上的AI模型解决多领域复杂任务。
AI.motivation: 现有AI模型无法独立处理跨领域复杂任务，而大语言模型在语言理解和推理方面表现出色，可通过语言接口协调其他模型。
AI.method: 使用ChatGPT进行任务规划，根据Hugging Face模型的功能描述选择模型，执行子任务并汇总结果。
AI.result: 在语言、视觉、语音等多模态任务中取得显著效果，验证了跨模型协作的可行性。
AI.conclusion: 该方法通过结合LLM的控制能力与Hugging Face的模型库，为实现通用人工智能提供了新路径。

authors_text: Jaehwan Jeong,Katherine Tian,Andrew Li,Sina Hartung,Fardad Behzadi,Juan Calle,David Osayande,Michael Pohlen,Subathra Adithan,Pranav Rajpurkar
title: Multimodal Image-Text Matching Improves Retrieval-based Chest X-Ray Report Generation
title_zh: 多模态图像-文本匹配改进基于检索的胸部 X 射线报告生成
abstract: Automated generation of clinically accurate radiology reports can improve patient care. Previous report generation methods that rely on image captioning models often generate incoherent and incorrect text due to their lack of relevant domain knowledge, while retrieval-based attempts frequently retrieve reports that are irrelevant to the input image. In this work, we propose Contrastive X-Ray REport Match (X-REM), a novel retrieval-based radiology report generation module that uses an image-text matching score to measure the similarity of a chest X-ray image and radiology report for report retrieval. We observe that computing the image-text matching score with a language-image model can effectively capture the fine-grained interaction between image and text that is often lost when using cosine similarity. X-REM outperforms multiple prior radiology report generation modules in terms of both natural language and clinical metrics. Human evaluation of the generated reports suggests that X-REM increased the number of zero-error reports and decreased the average error severity compared to the baseline retrieval approach. Our code is available at: https://github.com/rajpurkarlab/X-REM
summary: Automated generation of clinically accurate radiology reports can improve patient care. Previous report generation methods that rely on image captioning models often generate incoherent and incorrect text due to their lack of relevant domain knowledge, while retrieval-based attempts frequently retrieve reports that are irrelevant to the input image. In this work, we propose Contrastive X-Ray REport Match (X-REM), a novel retrieval-based radiology report generation module that uses an image-text matching score to measure the similarity of a chest X-ray image and radiology report for report retrieval. We observe that computing the image-text matching score with a language-image model can effectively capture the fine-grained interaction between image and text that is often lost when using cosine similarity. X-REM outperforms multiple prior radiology report generation modules in terms of both natural language and clinical metrics. Human evaluation of the generated reports suggests that X-REM increased the number of zero-error reports and decreased the average error severity compared to the baseline retrieval approach. Our code is available at: https://github.com/rajpurkarlab/X-REM
abstract_zh: 自动生成临床准确的放射学报告可以改善患者护理。以前依赖图像字幕模型的报告生成方法由于缺乏相关领域知识，经常生成不连贯且不正确的文本，而基于检索的尝试经常检索与输入图像无关的报告。在这项工作中，我们提出了对比 X 射线报告匹配（X-REM），这是一种新颖的基于检索的放射学报告生成模块，它使用图像文本匹配分数来测量胸部 X 射线图像和放射学报告的相似性以进行报告检索。我们观察到，使用语言图像模型计算图像文本匹配分数可以有效地捕获图像和文本之间的细粒度交互，而使用余弦相似度时经常会丢失这种交互。 X-REM 在自然语言和临床指标方面优于多个先前的放射学报告生成模块。对生成报告的人工评估表明，与基线检索方法相比，X-REM 增加了零错误报告的数量并降低了平均错误严重性。我们的代码位于：https://github.com/rajpurkarlab/X-REM

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于多模态图像-文本匹配的检索式胸部X光报告生成方法X-REM，通过改进图像与文本的相似度计算来提升报告生成的准确性。
AI.motivation: 现有基于图像描述生成的报告方法因缺乏领域知识导致文本不连贯或错误，而传统检索方法常返回与输入图像无关的报告，需改进报告生成的准确性与相关性。
AI.method: 设计X-REM模块，利用语言-图像模型计算图像-文本匹配分数，捕捉图像与文本间的细粒度交互，替代传统余弦相似度计算，提升检索效果。
AI.result: X-REM在自然语言与临床指标上优于多种现有方法，人类评估显示其生成报告的零错误率提高且平均错误严重程度降低。
AI.conclusion: 通过多模态图像-文本匹配改进检索方法，能有效提升胸部X光报告生成的准确性与临床适用性。

authors_text: Nico Daheim,Nouha Dziri,Mrinmaya Sachan,Iryna Gurevych,Edoardo M. Ponti
title: Elastic Weight Removal for Faithful and Abstractive Dialogue Generation
title_zh: 弹性权重去除以生成忠实和抽象的对话
abstract: Ideally, dialogue systems should generate responses that are faithful to the knowledge contained in relevant documents. However, many models generate hallucinated responses instead that contradict it or contain unverifiable information. To mitigate such undesirable behaviour, it has been proposed to fine-tune a `negative expert' on negative examples and subtract its parameters from those of a pre-trained model. However, intuitively, this does not take into account that some parameters are more responsible than others in causing hallucinations. Thus, we propose to weigh their individual importance via (an approximation of) the Fisher Information matrix, which measures the uncertainty of their estimate. We call this method Elastic Weight Removal (EWR). We evaluate our method -- using different variants of Flan-T5 as a backbone language model -- on multiple datasets for information-seeking dialogue generation and compare our method with state-of-the-art techniques for faithfulness, such as CTRL, Quark, DExperts, and Noisy Channel reranking. Extensive automatic and human evaluation shows that EWR systematically increases faithfulness at minor costs in terms of other metrics. However, we notice that only discouraging hallucinations may increase extractiveness, i.e. shallow copy-pasting of document spans, which can be undesirable. Hence, as a second main contribution, we show that our method can be extended to simultaneously discourage hallucinations and extractive responses. We publicly release the code for reproducing EWR and all baselines.
summary: Ideally, dialogue systems should generate responses that are faithful to the knowledge contained in relevant documents. However, many models generate hallucinated responses instead that contradict it or contain unverifiable information. To mitigate such undesirable behaviour, it has been proposed to fine-tune a `negative expert' on negative examples and subtract its parameters from those of a pre-trained model. However, intuitively, this does not take into account that some parameters are more responsible than others in causing hallucinations. Thus, we propose to weigh their individual importance via (an approximation of) the Fisher Information matrix, which measures the uncertainty of their estimate. We call this method Elastic Weight Removal (EWR). We evaluate our method -- using different variants of Flan-T5 as a backbone language model -- on multiple datasets for information-seeking dialogue generation and compare our method with state-of-the-art techniques for faithfulness, such as CTRL, Quark, DExperts, and Noisy Channel reranking. Extensive automatic and human evaluation shows that EWR systematically increases faithfulness at minor costs in terms of other metrics. However, we notice that only discouraging hallucinations may increase extractiveness, i.e. shallow copy-pasting of document spans, which can be undesirable. Hence, as a second main contribution, we show that our method can be extended to simultaneously discourage hallucinations and extractive responses. We publicly release the code for reproducing EWR and all baselines.
abstract_zh: 理想情况下，对话系统应该生成忠实于相关文档中包含的知识的响应。然而，许多模型会生成与事实相矛盾或包含无法验证信息的幻觉响应。为了减轻这种不良行为，有人建议对负面示例进行微调“负面专家”，并从预训练模型的参数中减去其参数。然而，直观上，这并没有考虑到某些参数比其他参数更容易引起幻觉。因此，我们建议通过费舍尔信息矩阵（的近似值）来衡量它们各自的重要性，该矩阵衡量了它们估计的不确定性。我们将此方法称为弹性重量去除 (EWR)。我们在多个数据集上评估我们的方法（使用 Flan-T5 的不同变体作为骨干语言模型）来生成信息寻求对话，并将我们的方法与最先进的忠实度技术（例如 CTRL、Quark、DExperts 和 Noisy Channel reranking）进行比较。广泛的自动和人工评估表明，EWR 在其他指标方面以较小的成本系统地提高了忠诚度。然而，我们注意到，只有令人沮丧的幻觉才能增加提取性，即文档跨度的浅层复制粘贴，这可能是不可取的。因此，作为第二个主要贡献，我们表明我们的方法可以扩展到同时阻止幻觉和提取反应。我们公开发布了复制 EWR 和所有基线的代码。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种名为Elastic Weight Removal (EWR)的方法，旨在通过调整预训练模型参数来提升对话生成系统的忠实度，减少幻觉和提取性响应。
AI.motivation: 现有对话系统常生成与文档矛盾的幻觉响应，传统方法通过减去负专家参数来缓解问题，但未考虑不同参数对幻觉的贡献差异，导致效果有限。
AI.method: 利用费舍尔信息矩阵近似衡量参数不确定性，按重要性调整参数权重，结合Flan-T5模型进行多数据集评估，并扩展方法以同时抑制幻觉和提取性响应。
AI.result: EWR在自动和人工评估中显著提升忠实度，仅小幅牺牲其他指标；但单纯抑制幻觉可能加剧提取性响应，扩展方法有效平衡了两者。
AI.conclusion: EWR通过参数重要性调整有效提升对话生成的忠实度，但需注意抑制幻觉与提取性响应的平衡，代码已开源供复现。

search_text: Elastic Weight Removal for Faithful and Abstractive Dialogue Generation 弹性权重去除以生成忠实和抽象的对话 Ideally, dialogue systems should generate responses that are faithful to the knowledge contained in relevant documents. However, many models generate hallucinated responses instead that contradict it or contain unverifiable information. To mitigate such undesirable behaviour, it has been proposed to fine-tune a `negative expert' on negative examples and subtract its parameters from those of a pre-trained model. However, intuitively, this does not take into account that some parameters are more responsible than others in causing hallucinations. Thus, we propose to weigh their individual importance via (an approximation of) the Fisher Information matrix, which measures the uncertainty of their estimate. We call this method Elastic Weight Removal (EWR). We evaluate our method -- using different variants of Flan-T5 as a backbone language model -- on multiple datasets for information-seeking dialogue generation and compare our method with state-of-the-art techniques for faithfulness, such as CTRL, Quark, DExperts, and Noisy Channel reranking. Extensive automatic and human evaluation shows that EWR systematically increases faithfulness at minor costs in terms of other metrics. However, we notice that only discouraging hallucinations may increase extractiveness, i.e. shallow copy-pasting of document spans, which can be undesirable. Hence, as a second main contribution, we show that our method can be extended to simultaneously discourage hallucinations and extractive responses. We publicly release the code for reproducing EWR and all baselines. 理想情况下，对话系统应该生成忠实于相关文档中包含的知识的响应。然而，许多模型会生成与事实相矛盾或包含无法验证信息的幻觉响应。为了减轻这种不良行为，有人建议对负面示例进行微调“负面专家”，并从预训练模型的参数中减去其参数。然而，直观上，这并没有考虑到某些参数比其他参数更容易引起幻觉。因此，我们建议通过费舍尔信息矩阵（的近似值）来衡量它们各自的重要性，该矩阵衡量了它们估计的不确定性。我们将此方法称为弹性重量去除 (EWR)。我们在多个数据集上评估我们的方法（使用 Flan-T5 的不同变体作为骨干语言模型）来生成信息寻求对话，并将我们的方法与最先进的忠实度技术（例如 CTRL、Quark、DExperts 和 Noisy Channel reranking）进行比较。广泛的自动和人工评估表明，EWR 在其他指标方面以较小的成本系统地提高了忠诚度。然而，我们注意到，只有令人沮丧的幻觉才能增加提取性，即文档跨度的浅层复制粘贴，这可能是不可取的。因此，作为第二个主要贡献，我们表明我们的方法可以扩展到同时阻止幻觉和提取反应。我们公开发布了复制 EWR 和所有基线的代码。 本文提出了一种名为Elastic Weight Removal (EWR)的方法，旨在通过调整预训练模型参数来提升对话生成系统的忠实度，减少幻觉和提取性响应。 Nico Daheim,Nouha Dziri,Mrinmaya Sachan,Iryna Gurevych,Edoardo M. authors_text: Md Saiful Islam,Wasifur Rahman,Abdelrahman Abdelkader,Phillip T. Yang,Sangwu Lee,Jamie L. Adams,Ruth B. Schneider,E. Ray Dorsey,Ehsan Hoque
title: Using AI to Measure Parkinson's Disease Severity at Home
title_zh: 使用人工智能在家测量帕金森病的严重程度
abstract: We present an artificial intelligence system to remotely assess the motor performance of individuals with Parkinson's disease (PD). Participants performed a motor task (i.e., tapping fingers) in front of a webcam, and data from 250 global participants were rated by three expert neurologists following the Movement Disorder Society Unified Parkinson's Disease Rating Scale (MDS-UPDRS). The neurologists' ratings were highly reliable, with an intra-class correlation coefficient (ICC) of 0.88. We developed computer algorithms to obtain objective measurements that align with the MDS-UPDRS guideline and are strongly correlated with the neurologists' ratings. Our machine learning model trained on these measures outperformed an MDS-UPDRS certified rater, with a mean absolute error (MAE) of 0.59 compared to the rater's MAE of 0.79. However, the model performed slightly worse than the expert neurologists (0.53 MAE). The methodology can be replicated for similar motor tasks, providing the possibility of evaluating individuals with PD and other movement disorders remotely, objectively, and in areas with limited access to neurological care.
summary: We present an artificial intelligence system to remotely assess the motor performance of individuals with Parkinson's disease (PD). Participants performed a motor task (i.e., tapping fingers) in front of a webcam, and data from 250 global participants were rated by three expert neurologists following the Movement Disorder Society Unified Parkinson's Disease Rating Scale (MDS-UPDRS). The neurologists' ratings were highly reliable, with an intra-class correlation coefficient (ICC) of 0.88. We developed computer algorithms to obtain objective measurements that align with the MDS-UPDRS guideline and are strongly correlated with the neurologists' ratings. Our machine learning model trained on these measures outperformed an MDS-UPDRS certified rater, with a mean absolute error (MAE) of 0.59 compared to the rater's MAE of 0.79. However, the model performed slightly worse than the expert neurologists (0.53 MAE). The methodology can be replicated for similar motor tasks, providing the possibility of evaluating individuals with PD and other movement disorders remotely, objectively, and in areas with limited access to neurological care.
abstract_zh: 我们提出了一种人工智能系统来远程评估帕金森病 (PD) 患者的运动表现。参与者在网络摄像头前执行一项运动任务（即敲击手指），来自全球 250 名参与者的数据由三位专家神经科医生按照运动障碍协会统一帕金森病评分量表 (MDS-UPDRS) 进行评分。神经科医生的评分高度可靠，组内相关系数 (ICC) 为 0.88。我们开发了计算机算法来获得符合 MDS-UPDRS 指南并与神经科医生的评分密切相关的客观测量结果。我们根据这些指标进行训练的机器学习模型的表现优于 MDS-UPDRS 认证的评估者，平均绝对误差 (MAE) 为 0.59，而评估者的 MAE 为 0.79。然而，该模型的表现比神经科医生专家的表现稍差（0.53 MAE）。该方法可以复制用于类似的运动任务，从而为在神经科护理机会有限的地区远程、客观地评估患有帕金森病和其他运动障碍的个体提供了可能性。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种利用人工智能通过网络摄像头远程评估帕金森病患者运动表现的系统。
AI.motivation: 为了解决帕金森病患者在缺乏神经科医疗资源的地区难以获得客观评估的问题，提供一种远程、客观的评估方法。
AI.method: 通过网络摄像头采集250名全球参与者执行手指敲击任务的视频数据，由三位神经科专家根据MDS-UPDRS量表评分，开发与该量表对齐的计算机算法并训练机器学习模型。
AI.result: 神经科专家评分一致性为ICC 0.88，AI模型的平均绝对误差(MAE)为0.59，优于认证评分者(0.79)，但略逊于专家(0.53)。
AI.conclusion: 该方法可复制用于其他运动障碍的远程客观评估，尤其适用于医疗资源匮乏地区。

authors_text: Zhexin Liang,Chongyi Li,Shangchen Zhou,Ruicheng Feng,Chen Change Loy
title: Iterative Prompt Learning for Unsupervised Backlit Image Enhancement
title_zh: 无监督背光图像增强的迭代提示学习
comment: Accepted to ICCV 2023 as Oral. Project page: https://zhexinliang.github.io/CLIP_LIT_page/
comments: Accepted to ICCV 2023 as Oral. Project page: https://zhexinliang.github.io/CLIP_LIT_page/
abstract: We propose a novel unsupervised backlit image enhancement method, abbreviated as CLIP-LIT, by exploring the potential of Contrastive Language-Image Pre-Training (CLIP) for pixel-level image enhancement. We show that the open-world CLIP prior not only aids in distinguishing between backlit and well-lit images, but also in perceiving heterogeneous regions with different luminance, facilitating the optimization of the enhancement network. Unlike high-level and image manipulation tasks, directly applying CLIP to enhancement tasks is non-trivial, owing to the difficulty in finding accurate prompts. To solve this issue, we devise a prompt learning framework that first learns an initial prompt pair by constraining the text-image similarity between the prompt (negative/positive sample) and the corresponding image (backlit image/well-lit image) in the CLIP latent space. Then, we train the enhancement network based on the text-image similarity between the enhanced result and the initial prompt pair. To further improve the accuracy of the initial prompt pair, we iteratively fine-tune the prompt learning framework to reduce the distribution gaps between the backlit images, enhanced results, and well-lit images via rank learning, boosting the enhancement performance. Our method alternates between updating the prompt learning framework and enhancement network until visually pleasing results are achieved. Extensive experiments demonstrate that our method outperforms state-of-the-art methods in terms of visual quality and generalization ability, without requiring any paired data.
summary: We propose a novel unsupervised backlit image enhancement method, abbreviated as CLIP-LIT, by exploring the potential of Contrastive Language-Image Pre-Training (CLIP) for pixel-level image enhancement. We show that the open-world CLIP prior not only aids in distinguishing between backlit and well-lit images, but also in perceiving heterogeneous regions with different luminance, facilitating the optimization of the enhancement network. Unlike high-level and image manipulation tasks, directly applying CLIP to enhancement tasks is non-trivial, owing to the difficulty in finding accurate prompts. To solve this issue, we devise a prompt learning framework that first learns an initial prompt pair by constraining the text-image similarity between the prompt (negative/positive sample) and the corresponding image (backlit image/well-lit image) in the CLIP latent space. Then, we train the enhancement network based on the text-image similarity between the enhanced result and the initial prompt pair. To further improve the accuracy of the initial prompt pair, we iteratively fine-tune the prompt learning framework to reduce the distribution gaps between the backlit images, enhanced results, and well-lit images via rank learning, boosting the enhancement performance. Our method alternates between updating the prompt learning framework and enhancement network until visually pleasing results are achieved. Extensive experiments demonstrate that our method outperforms state-of-the-art methods in terms of visual quality and generalization ability, without requiring any paired data.
abstract_zh: 通过探索对比语言图像预训练（CLIP）在像素级图像增强方面的潜力，我们提出了一种新颖的无监督背光图像增强方法，缩写为 CLIP-LIT。我们表明，开放世界 CLIP 先验不仅有助于区分背光图像和光线充足的图像，而且还有助于感知具有不同亮度的异质区域，从而促进增强网络的优化。与高级任务和图像处理任务不同，直接将 CLIP 应用于增强任务并非易事，因为很难找到准确的提示。为了解决这个问题，我们设计了一个提示学习框架，首先通过限制 CLIP 潜在空间中提示（负样本/正样本）与相应图像（背光图像/光线充足图像）之间的文本图像相似度来学习初始提示对。然后，我们根据增强结果和初始提示对之间的文本图像相似度来训练增强网络。为了进一步提高初始提示对的准确性，我们迭代地微调提示学习框架，通过排名学习来减少背光图像、增强结果和光线充足图像之间的分布差距，从而提高增强性能。我们的方法在更新即时学习框架和增强网络之间交替，直到获得视觉上令人愉悦的结果。大量的实验表明，我们的方法在视觉质量和泛化能力方面优于最先进的方法，而无需任何配对数据。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种无需配对数据的无监督背光图像增强方法CLIP-LIT，通过迭代提示学习框架优化增强网络。
AI.motivation: 现有背光图像增强方法依赖配对数据且难以准确生成提示词，而CLIP的开放世界先验可区分背光与正常光照图像并感知亮度异质区域。
AI.method: 构建CLIP-LIT框架：1) 通过约束CLIP潜在空间中文本-图像相似性学习初始提示对；2) 基于增强结果与初始提示对的相似性训练增强网络；3) 迭代优化提示学习框架以缩小背光图像、增强结果与正常图像间的分布差距。
AI.result: 实验表明该方法在视觉质量和泛化能力上优于现有最先进的方法，且无需任何配对数据。
AI.conclusion: 通过迭代提示学习与CLIP先验结合，实现了无需配对数据的背光图像增强，为无监督图像增强提供了新思路。

search_text: Iterative Prompt Learning for Unsupervised Backlit Image Enhancement 无监督背光图像增强的迭代提示学习 We propose a novel unsupervised backlit image enhancement method, abbreviated as CLIP-LIT, by exploring the potential of Contrastive Language-Image Pre-Training (CLIP) for pixel-level image enhancement. We show that the open-world CLIP prior not only aids in distinguishing between backlit and well-lit images, but also in perceiving heterogeneous regions with different luminance, facilitating the optimization of the enhancement network. Unlike high-level and image manipulation tasks, directly applying CLIP to enhancement tasks is non-trivial, owing to the difficulty in finding accurate prompts. To solve this issue, we devise a prompt learning framework that first learns an initial prompt pair by constraining the text-image similarity between the prompt (negative/positive sample) and the corresponding image (backlit image/well-lit image) in the CLIP latent space. Then, we train the enhancement network based on the text-image similarity between the enhanced result and the initial prompt pair. To further improve the accuracy of the initial prompt pair, we iteratively fine-tune the prompt learning framework to reduce the distribution gaps between the backlit images, enhanced results, and well-lit images via rank learning, boosting the enhancement performance. Our method alternates between updating the prompt learning framework and enhancement network until visually pleasing results are achieved. Extensive experiments demonstrate that our method outperforms state-of-the-art methods in terms of visual quality and generalization ability, without requiring any paired data. 通过探索对比语言图像预训练（CLIP）在像素级图像增强方面的潜力，我们提出了一种新颖的无监督背光图像增强方法，缩写为 CLIP-LIT。我们表明，开放世界 CLIP 先验不仅有助于区分背光图像和光线充足的图像，而且还有助于感知具有不同亮度的异质区域，从而促进增强网络的优化。与高级任务和图像处理任务不同，直接将 CLIP 应用于增强任务并非易事，因为很难找到准确的提示。为了解决这个问题，我们设计了一个提示学习框架，首先通过限制 CLIP 潜在空间中提示（负样本/正样本）与相应图像（背光图像/光线充足图像）之间的文本图像相似度来学习初始提示对。然后，我们根据增强结果和初始提示对之间的文本图像相似度来训练增强网络。为了进一步提高初始提示对的准确性，我们迭代地微调提示学习框架，通过排名学习来减少背光图像、增强结果和光线充足图像之间的分布差距，从而提高增强性能。我们的方法在更新即时学习框架和增强网络之间交替，直到获得视觉上令人愉悦的结果。大量的实验表明，我们的方法在视觉质量和泛化能力方面优于最先进的方法，而无需任何配对数据。 本文提出一种无需配对数据的无监督背光图像增强方法CLIP-LIT，通过迭代提示学习框架优化增强网络。 Zhexin Liang,Chongyi Li,Shangchen Zhou,Ruicheng Feng,Chen Change Loy cs.CV

authors_text: Qinkai Zheng,Xiao Xia,Xu Zou,Yuxiao Dong,Shan Wang,Yufei Xue,Zihan Wang,Lei Shen,Andi Wang,Yang Li,Teng Su,Zhilin Yang,Jie Tang
title: CodeGeeX: A Pre-Trained Model for Code Generation with Multilingual Benchmarking on HumanEval-X
title_zh: CodeGeeX：在 HumanEval-X 上进行多语言基准测试的代码生成预训练模型
abstract: Large pre-trained code generation models, such as OpenAI Codex, can generate syntax- and function-correct code, making the coding of programmers more productive and our pursuit of artificial general intelligence closer. In this paper, we introduce CodeGeeX, a multilingual model with 13 billion parameters for code generation. CodeGeeX is pre-trained on 850 billion tokens of 23 programming languages as of June 2022. Our extensive experiments suggest that CodeGeeX outperforms multilingual code models of similar scale for both the tasks of code generation and translation on HumanEval-X. Building upon HumanEval (Python only), we develop the HumanEval-X benchmark for evaluating multilingual models by hand-writing the solutions in C++, Java, JavaScript, and Go. In addition, we build CodeGeeX-based extensions on Visual Studio Code, JetBrains, and Cloud Studio, generating 4.7 billion tokens for tens of thousands of active users per week. Our user study demonstrates that CodeGeeX can help to increase coding efficiency for 83.4% of its users. Finally, CodeGeeX is publicly accessible and in Sep. 2022, we open-sourced its code, model weights (the version of 850B tokens), API, extensions, and HumanEval-X at https://github.com/THUDM/CodeGeeX.
summary: Large pre-trained code generation models, such as OpenAI Codex, can generate syntax- and function-correct code, making the coding of programmers more productive and our pursuit of artificial general intelligence closer. In this paper, we introduce CodeGeeX, a multilingual model with 13 billion parameters for code generation. CodeGeeX is pre-trained on 850 billion tokens of 23 programming languages as of June 2022. Our extensive experiments suggest that CodeGeeX outperforms multilingual code models of similar scale for both the tasks of code generation and translation on HumanEval-X. Building upon HumanEval (Python only), we develop the HumanEval-X benchmark for evaluating multilingual models by hand-writing the solutions in C++, Java, JavaScript, and Go. In addition, we build CodeGeeX-based extensions on Visual Studio Code, JetBrains, and Cloud Studio, generating 4.7 billion tokens for tens of thousands of active users per week. Our user study demonstrates that CodeGeeX can help to increase coding efficiency for 83.4% of its users. Finally, CodeGeeX is publicly accessible and in Sep. 2022, we open-sourced its code, model weights (the version of 850B tokens), API, extensions, and HumanEval-X at https://github.com/THUDM/CodeGeeX.
abstract_zh: 大型预训练代码生成模型，例如 OpenAI Codex，可以生成语法和功能正确的代码，使程序员的编码更加高效，也更加接近我们对通用人工智能的追求。在本文中，我们介绍了 CodeGeeX，这是一个具有 130 亿个代码生成参数的多语言模型。截至 2022 年 6 月，CodeGeeX 在 23 种编程语言的 8500 亿个令牌上进行了预训练。我们的大量实验表明，在 HumanEval-X 上的代码生成和翻译任务中，CodeGeeX 的性能优于类似规模的多语言代码模型。在 HumanEval（仅限 Python）的基础上，我们开发了 HumanEval-X 基准，通过用 C++、Java、JavaScript 和 Go 手写解决方案来评估多语言模型。此外，我们还在 Visual Studio Code、JetBrains 和 Cloud Studio 上构建基于 CodeGeeX 的扩展，每周为数万活跃用户生成 47 亿个代币。我们的用户研究表明 CodeGeeX 可以帮助提高 83.4% 的用户的编码效率。最后，CodeGeeX 是可公开访问的，我们于 2022 年 9 月在 https://github.com/THUDM/CodeGeeX 开源了其代码、模型权重（850B 代币版本）、API、扩展和 HumanEval-X。

AI.tldr: 本文介绍了一个名为CodeGeeX的多语言代码生成预训练模型，该模型在HumanEval-X基准测试中表现出色，并提供了基于该模型的开发工具扩展。
AI.motivation: 尽管现有大型代码生成模型（如OpenAI Codex）能生成正确代码，但缺乏针对多语言场景的系统性研究和基准测试，且实际应用效果需进一步验证。
AI.method: 基于8500亿token的23种编程语言语料训练130亿参数模型，构建多语言HumanEval-X基准（含C++/Java/JavaScript/Go人工编写解决方案），并开发VS Code等集成工具。
AI.result: CodeGeeX在代码生成和翻译任务上优于同类多语言模型，周活跃用户超万人，83.4%用户反馈提升编码效率，模型参数与代码已开源。
AI.conclusion: CodeGeeX通过大规模预训练和多语言基准测试验证了其有效性，为代码生成领域提供了可复用的开源工具链和评估体系。

authors_text: Shijie Wu,Ozan Irsoy,Steven Lu,Vadim Dabravolski,Mark Dredze,Sebastian Gehrmann,Prabhanjan Kambadur,David Rosenberg,Gideon Mann
title: BloombergGPT: A Large Language Model for Finance
title_zh: BloombergGPT：大型金融语言模型
comment: Updated to include Training Chronicles (Appendix C)
comments: Updated to include Training Chronicles (Appendix C)
abstract: The use of NLP in the realm of financial technology is broad and complex, with applications ranging from sentiment analysis and named entity recognition to question answering. Large Language Models (LLMs) have been shown to be effective on a variety of tasks; however, no LLM specialized for the financial domain has been reported in literature. In this work, we present BloombergGPT, a 50 billion parameter language model that is trained on a wide range of financial data. We construct a 363 billion token dataset based on Bloomberg's extensive data sources, perhaps the largest domain-specific dataset yet, augmented with 345 billion tokens from general purpose datasets. We validate BloombergGPT on standard LLM benchmarks, open financial benchmarks, and a suite of internal benchmarks that most accurately reflect our intended usage. Our mixed dataset training leads to a model that outperforms existing models on financial tasks by significant margins without sacrificing performance on general LLM benchmarks. Additionally, we explain our modeling choices, training process, and evaluation methodology. We release Training Chronicles (Appendix C) detailing our experience in training BloombergGPT.
summary: The use of NLP in the realm of financial technology is broad and complex, with applications ranging from sentiment analysis and named entity recognition to question answering. Large Language Models (LLMs) have been shown to be effective on a variety of tasks; however, no LLM specialized for the financial domain has been reported in literature. In this work, we present BloombergGPT, a 50 billion parameter language model that is trained on a wide range of financial data. We construct a 363 billion token dataset based on Bloomberg's extensive data sources, perhaps the largest domain-specific dataset yet, augmented with 345 billion tokens from general purpose datasets. We validate BloombergGPT on standard LLM benchmarks, open financial benchmarks, and a suite of internal benchmarks that most accurately reflect our intended usage. Our mixed dataset training leads to a model that outperforms existing models on financial tasks by significant margins without sacrificing performance on general LLM benchmarks. Additionally, we explain our modeling choices, training process, and evaluation methodology. We release Training Chronicles (Appendix C) detailing our experience in training BloombergGPT.
abstract_zh: NLP 在金融技术领域的应用广泛而复杂，应用范围从情感分析、命名实体识别到问题解答。大型语言模型 (LLM) 已被证明对各种任务都有效；然而，文献中尚未报道专门针对金融领域的法学硕士。在这项工作中，我们提出了 BloombergGPT，这是一个经过广泛金融数据训练的 500 亿参数语言模型。我们基于 Bloomberg 广泛的数据源构建了 3630 亿个代币数据集，这可能是迄今为止最大的特定领​​域数据集，并增加了来自通用数据集的 3450 亿个代币。我们根据标准 LLM 基准、开放式金融基准以及一套最准确反映我们预期用途的内部基准来验证 BloombergGPT。我们的混合数据集训练产生的模型在财务任务上明显优于现有模型，而不会牺牲一般法学硕士基准的性能。此外，我们还解释了我们的建模选择、训练过程和评估方法。我们发布了培训纪事（附录 C），详细介绍了我们培训 BloombergGPT 的经验。

AI.tldr: 本文提出BloombergGPT，一个基于500亿参数的大型语言模型，专门针对金融领域进行训练和优化。
AI.motivation: 尽管大型语言模型（LLMs）在多种任务中表现优异，但现有研究缺乏针对金融领域的专用模型，而金融数据的复杂性需要专门的处理方法。
AI.method: 构建包含3630亿金融领域专用标记和3450亿通用数据标记的混合数据集，训练500亿参数模型，并通过标准LLM基准、公开金融基准及内部基准进行验证。
AI.result: BloombergGPT在金融任务上显著优于现有模型，同时保持通用LLM基准的性能，验证了混合数据训练的有效性。
AI.conclusion: 通过专用数据集和混合训练策略，BloombergGPT成功实现了金融领域的高性能语言模型，相关训练细节已公开共享。

search_text: BloombergGPT: A Large Language Model for Finance BloombergGPT：大型金融语言模型 The use of NLP in the realm of financial technology is broad and complex, with applications ranging from sentiment analysis and named entity recognition to question answering. Large Language Models (LLMs) have been shown to be effective on a variety of tasks; however, no LLM specialized for the financial domain has been reported in literature. In this work, we present BloombergGPT, a 50 billion parameter language model that is trained on a wide range of financial data. We construct a 363 billion token dataset based on Bloomberg's extensive data sources, perhaps the largest domain-specific dataset yet, augmented with 345 billion tokens from general purpose datasets. We validate BloombergGPT on standard LLM benchmarks, open financial benchmarks, and a suite of internal benchmarks that most accurately reflect our intended usage. Our mixed dataset training leads to a model that outperforms existing models on financial tasks by significant margins without sacrificing performance on general LLM benchmarks. Additionally, we explain our modeling choices, training process, and evaluation methodology. We release Training Chronicles (Appendix C) detailing our experience in training BloombergGPT. authors_text: Panagiotis Mougkogiannis,Andrew Adamatzky
title: Light induced spiking of proteinoids
title_zh: 光诱导类蛋白的尖峰
abstract: Proteinoids, or thermal proteins, are produced by heating amino acids to their melting point and initiation of polymerisation to produce polymeric chains. In aqueous solutions proteinoids swell into hollow microspheres. These microspheres produce endogenous burst of electrical potential spikes and change patterns of their electrical activity in response to illumination. We report results of detailed investigation on the effects of white cold light on the spiking of proteinoids. We study how different types and intensities of light determine proteinoids' spiking amplitude, period, and pattern. The results of this study will be utilised to evaluate proteinoids for their potential as optical sensors and their application in unconventional computing.
summary: Proteinoids, or thermal proteins, are produced by heating amino acids to their melting point and initiation of polymerisation to produce polymeric chains. In aqueous solutions proteinoids swell into hollow microspheres. These microspheres produce endogenous burst of electrical potential spikes and change patterns of their electrical activity in response to illumination. We report results of detailed investigation on the effects of white cold light on the spiking of proteinoids. We study how different types and intensities of light determine proteinoids' spiking amplitude, period, and pattern. The results of this study will be utilised to evaluate proteinoids for their potential as optical sensors and their application in unconventional computing.
abstract_zh: 类蛋白或热蛋白是通过将氨基酸加热至熔点并引发聚合以产生聚合链而产生的。在水溶液中，类蛋白膨胀成空心微球。这些微球产生内源性的电势尖峰爆发，并响应光照而改变其电活动模式。我们报告了白冷光对类蛋白尖峰影响的详细研究结果。我们研究不同类型和强度的光如何决定类蛋白的尖峰幅度、周期和模式。这项研究的结果将用于评估类蛋白作为光学传感器的潜力及其在非常规计算中的应用。

AI.tldr: 研究光照如何影响蛋白体的电位尖峰特性，评估其作为光学传感器和非传统计算组件的潜力。
AI.motivation: 探索蛋白体在光控传感和计算中的应用可能性，需明确光照对其电活动的影响机制。
AI.method: 通过调控白光类型与强度，观测蛋白体微球在光照下的电位尖峰幅度、周期及模式变化。
AI.result: 发现光的类型和强度显著影响蛋白体尖峰的幅度、周期及模式，为光学传感应用提供数据支持。
AI.conclusion: 蛋白体的光敏电活动特性表明其可作为光学传感器或非传统计算系统的潜在基础材料。

authors_text: Yuting Gao,Jinfeng Liu,Zihan Xu,Tong Wu Enwei Zhang,Wei Liu,Jie Yang,Ke Li,Xing Sun
title: SoftCLIP: Softer Cross-modal Alignment Makes CLIP Stronger
title_zh: SoftCLIP：更柔和的跨模态对准使 CLIP 更强
abstract: During the preceding biennium, vision-language pre-training has achieved noteworthy success on several downstream tasks. Nevertheless, acquiring high-quality image-text pairs, where the pairs are entirely exclusive of each other, remains a challenging task, and noise exists in the commonly used datasets. To address this issue, we propose SoftCLIP, a novel approach that relaxes the strict one-to-one constraint and achieves a soft cross-modal alignment by introducing a softened target, which is generated from the fine-grained intra-modal self-similarity. The intra-modal guidance is indicative to enable two pairs have some local similarities and model many-to-many relationships between the two modalities. Besides, since the positive still dominates in the softened target distribution, we disentangle the negatives in the distribution to further boost the relation alignment with the negatives in the cross-modal learning. Extensive experiments demonstrate the effectiveness of SoftCLIP. In particular, on ImageNet zero-shot classification task, using CC3M/CC12M as pre-training dataset, SoftCLIP brings a top-1 accuracy improvement of 6.8%/7.2% over the CLIP baseline.
summary: During the preceding biennium, vision-language pre-training has achieved noteworthy success on several downstream tasks. Nevertheless, acquiring high-quality image-text pairs, where the pairs are entirely exclusive of each other, remains a challenging task, and noise exists in the commonly used datasets. To address this issue, we propose SoftCLIP, a novel approach that relaxes the strict one-to-one constraint and achieves a soft cross-modal alignment by introducing a softened target, which is generated from the fine-grained intra-modal self-similarity. The intra-modal guidance is indicative to enable two pairs have some local similarities and model many-to-many relationships between the two modalities. Besides, since the positive still dominates in the softened target distribution, we disentangle the negatives in the distribution to further boost the relation alignment with the negatives in the cross-modal learning. Extensive experiments demonstrate the effectiveness of SoftCLIP. In particular, on ImageNet zero-shot classification task, using CC3M/CC12M as pre-training dataset, SoftCLIP brings a top-1 accuracy improvement of 6.8%/7.2% over the CLIP baseline.
abstract_zh: 在上一个两年期中，视觉语言预训练在多项下游任务上取得了显着的成功。然而，获取高质量的图像文本对（其中图像文本对彼此完全互斥）仍然是一项具有挑战性的任务，并且常用数据集中存在噪声。为了解决这个问题，我们提出了 SoftCLIP，这是一种新方法，它放宽了严格的一对一约束，并通过引入软化目标来实现软跨模态对齐，该软化目标是根据细粒度模态内自相似性生成的。模态内指导表明使两对具有一些局部相似性并建模两种模态之间的多对多关系。此外，由于在软化的目标分布中正值仍然占主导地位，因此我们解开了分布中的负值，以进一步促进跨模态学习中与负值的关系对齐。大量实验证明了 SoftCLIP 的有效性。特别是，在 ImageNet 零样本分类任务上，使用 CC3M/CC12M 作为预训练数据集，SoftCLIP 比 CLIP 基线带来了 6.8%/7.2% 的 top-1 精度提升。

AI.tldr: 本文提出SoftCLIP方法，通过软化跨模态对齐来增强CLIP模型的性能。
AI.motivation: 高质量的图文对获取困难且常用数据集存在噪声，传统严格的一对一对齐约束难以适应实际场景。
AI.method: 引入软化目标生成机制，基于细粒度模态内自相似性实现软跨模态对齐，并通过分离负样本分布提升关系对齐效果。
AI.result: 在ImageNet零样本分类任务中，使用CC3M/CC12M预训练数据集时，SoftCLIP较CLIP基线分别提升6.8%/7.2%的Top-1准确率。
AI.conclusion: SoftCLIP通过软化跨模态对齐约束和负样本分离策略，有效提升了视觉-语言预训练模型的性能。

Besides, since the positive still dominates in the softened target distribution, we disentangle the negatives in the distribution to further boost the relation alignment with the negatives in the cross-modal learning. Extensive experiments demonstrate the effectiveness of SoftCLIP. In particular, on ImageNet zero-shot classification task, using CC3M/CC12M as pre-training dataset, SoftCLIP brings a top-1 accuracy improvement of 6.8%/7.2% over the CLIP baseline. 在上一个两年期中，视觉语言预训练在多项下游任务上取得了显着的成功。然而，获取高质量的图像文本对（其中图像文本对彼此完全互斥）仍然是一项具有挑战性的任务，并且常用数据集中存在噪声。为了解决这个问题，我们提出了 SoftCLIP，这是一种新方法，它放宽了严格的一对一约束，并通过引入软化目标来实现软跨模态对齐，该软化目标是根据细粒度模态内自相似性生成的。模态内指导表明使两对具有一些局部相似性并建模两种模态之间的多对多关系。此外，由于在软化的目标分布中正值仍然占主导地位，因此我们解开了分布中的负值，以进一步促进跨模态学习中与负值的关系对齐。大量实验证明了 SoftCLIP 的有效性。特别是，在 ImageNet 零样本分类任务上，使用 CC3M/CC12M 作为预训练数据集，SoftCLIP 比 CLIP 基线带来了 6.8%/7.2% 的 top-1 精度提升。 本文提出SoftCLIP方法，通过软化跨模态对齐来增强CLIP模型的性能。 Yuting Gao,Jinfeng Liu,Zihan Xu,Tong Wu Enwei Zhang,Wei Liu,Jie Yang,Ke Li,Xing Sun cs.CV,cs.AI

authors_text: Yuanfeng Ji,Zhe Chen,Enze Xie,Lanqing Hong,Xihui Liu,Zhaoqiang Liu,Tong Lu,Zhenguo Li,Ping Luo
title: DDP: Diffusion Model for Dense Visual Prediction
title_zh: DDP：密集视觉预测的扩散模型
abstract: We propose a simple, efficient, yet powerful framework for dense visual predictions based on the conditional diffusion pipeline. Our approach follows a "noise-to-map" generative paradigm for prediction by progressively removing noise from a random Gaussian distribution, guided by the image. The method, called DDP, efficiently extends the denoising diffusion process into the modern perception pipeline. Without task-specific design and architecture customization, DDP is easy to generalize to most dense prediction tasks, e.g., semantic segmentation and depth estimation. In addition, DDP shows attractive properties such as dynamic inference and uncertainty awareness, in contrast to previous single-step discriminative methods. We show top results on three representative tasks with six diverse benchmarks, without tricks, DDP achieves state-of-the-art or competitive performance on each task compared to the specialist counterparts. For example, semantic segmentation (83.9 mIoU on Cityscapes), BEV map segmentation (70.6 mIoU on nuScenes), and depth estimation (0.05 REL on KITTI). We hope that our approach will serve as a solid baseline and facilitate future research
summary: We propose a simple, efficient, yet powerful framework for dense visual predictions based on the conditional diffusion pipeline. Our approach follows a "noise-to-map" generative paradigm for prediction by progressively removing noise from a random Gaussian distribution, guided by the image. The method, called DDP, efficiently extends the denoising diffusion process into the modern perception pipeline. Without task-specific design and architecture customization, DDP is easy to generalize to most dense prediction tasks, e.g., semantic segmentation and depth estimation. In addition, DDP shows attractive properties such as dynamic inference and uncertainty awareness, in contrast to previous single-step discriminative methods. We show top results on three representative tasks with six diverse benchmarks, without tricks, DDP achieves state-of-the-art or competitive performance on each task compared to the specialist counterparts. For example, semantic segmentation (83.9 mIoU on Cityscapes), BEV map segmentation (70.6 mIoU on nuScenes), and depth estimation (0.05 REL on KITTI). We hope that our approach will serve as a solid baseline and facilitate future research
abstract_zh: 我们提出了一个简单、高效且强大的框架，用于基于条件扩散管道的密集视觉预测。我们的方法遵循“噪声到地图”生成范式，通过在图像的引导下逐步从随机高斯分布中去除噪声来进行预测。该方法称为 DDP，有效地将去噪扩散过程扩展到现代感知管道中。无需针对特定任务的设计和架构定制，DDP 很容易推广到最密集的预测任务，例如语义分割和深度估计。此外，与之前的单步判别方法相比，DDP 还表现出动态推理和不确定性意识等有吸引力的特性。我们在六个不同基准的三个代表性任务上显示了最佳结果，无需任何技巧，与专业同行相比，DDP 在每个任务上都实现了最先进的或有竞争力的性能。例如，语义分割（Cityscapes 上为 83.9 mIoU）、BEV 地图分割（nuScenes 上为 70.6 mIoU）和深度估计（KITTI 上为 0.05 REL）。我们希望我们的方法能够作为坚实的基线并促进未来的研究

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于条件扩散模型的密集视觉预测框架DDP，适用于语义分割、深度估计等任务。
AI.motivation: 现有密集预测任务通常需要任务特定的模型设计，缺乏通用性和效率，而扩散模型在生成任务中表现出强大潜力。
AI.method: 采用'噪声到地图'生成范式，通过逐步去除高斯噪声并结合图像引导，无需任务特定设计即可扩展到多种密集预测任务。
AI.result: 在Cityscapes、nuScenes和KITTI等六个基准测试中，DDP在语义分割（83.9 mIoU）、BEV地图分割（70.6 mIoU）和深度估计（0.05 REL）等任务中达到SOTA或竞争力结果。
AI.conclusion: DDP为密集视觉预测提供了通用且高效的基线方法，有助于推动未来相关研究。

authors_text: Chiara Gallese
title: The AI Act proposal: a new right to technical interpretability?
title_zh: 人工智能法案提案：技术可解释性的新权利？
comment: 10 pages, Information Society Law Center online event, 20-22 February 2023
comments: 10 pages, Information Society Law Center online event, 20-22 February 2023
abstract: The debate about the concept of the so called right to explanation in AI is the subject of a wealth of literature. It has focused, in the legal scholarship, on art. 22 GDPR and, in the technical scholarship, on techniques that help explain the output of a certain model (XAI). The purpose of this work is to investigate if the new provisions introduced by the proposal for a Regulation laying down harmonised rules on artificial intelligence (AI Act), in combination with Convention 108 plus and GDPR, are enough to indicate the existence of a right to technical explainability in the EU legal framework and, if not, whether the EU should include it in its current legislation. This is a preliminary work submitted to the online event organised by the Information Society Law Center and it will be later developed into a full paper.
summary: The debate about the concept of the so called right to explanation in AI is the subject of a wealth of literature. It has focused, in the legal scholarship, on art. 22 GDPR and, in the technical scholarship, on techniques that help explain the output of a certain model (XAI). The purpose of this work is to investigate if the new provisions introduced by the proposal for a Regulation laying down harmonised rules on artificial intelligence (AI Act), in combination with Convention 108 plus and GDPR, are enough to indicate the existence of a right to technical explainability in the EU legal framework and, if not, whether the EU should include it in its current legislation. This is a preliminary work submitted to the online event organised by the Information Society Law Center and it will be later developed into a full paper.
abstract_zh: 关于人工智能中所谓的解释权概念的争论是大量文献的主题。在法律学术方面，它的重点是艺术。 22 GDPR 以及技术学术中有助于解释特定模型 (XAI) 输出的技术。这项工作的目的是调查制定人工智能协调规则的法规（AI法案）提案中引入的新条款，与第108号附加公约和GDPR相结合，是否足以表明欧盟法律框架中存在技术可解释性权利，如果没有，欧盟是否应将其纳入其现行立法中。这是提交给信息社会法律中心组织的在线活动的初步工作，稍后将发展为完整的论文。

AI.tldr: 本文探讨欧盟《人工智能法案》提案是否确立了技术可解释性的新权利，并分析其与GDPR和Convention 108+的结合是否足够。
AI.motivation: 现有文献对AI的‘解释权’概念存在法律与技术层面的争议，需明确欧盟法律框架下是否应明确技术可解释性权利。
AI.method: 通过分析《人工智能法案》提案内容，并结合GDPR及Convention 108+条款进行法律与技术层面的综合评估。
AI.result: 当前提案与现有法规未明确确立技术可解释性权利，需进一步立法补充以完善欧盟AI治理框架。
AI.conclusion: 欧盟应考虑在现行法律中明确纳入技术可解释性权利，以应对AI应用中的透明度与 accountability 挑战。

authors_text: A. Emin Orhan
title: Recognition, recall, and retention of few-shot memories in large language models
title_zh: 大型语言模型中少数镜头记忆的识别、回忆和保留
abstract: The training of modern large language models (LLMs) takes place in a regime where most training examples are seen only a few times by the model during the course of training. What does a model remember about such examples seen only a few times during training and how long does that memory persist in the face of continuous training with new examples? Here, we investigate these questions through simple recognition, recall, and retention experiments with LLMs. In recognition experiments, we ask if the model can distinguish the seen example from a novel example; in recall experiments, we ask if the model can correctly recall the seen example when cued by a part of it; and in retention experiments, we periodically probe the model's memory for the original examples as the model is trained continuously with new examples. We find that a single exposure is generally sufficient for a model to achieve near perfect accuracy even in very challenging recognition experiments. We estimate that the recognition performance of even small language models easily exceeds human recognition performance reported in similar experiments with humans (Shepard, 1967). Achieving near perfect recall takes more exposures, but most models can do it in just 3 exposures. The flip side of this remarkable capacity for fast learning is that precise memories are quickly overwritten: recall performance for the original examples drops steeply over the first 10 training updates with new examples, followed by a more gradual decline. Even after 100K updates, however, some of the original examples are still recalled near perfectly. A qualitatively similar retention pattern has been observed in human long-term memory retention studies before (Bahrick, 1984). Finally, recognition is much more robust to interference than recall and memory for natural language sentences is generally superior to memory for stimuli without structure.
summary: The training of modern large language models (LLMs) takes place in a regime where most training examples are seen only a few times by the model during the course of training. What does a model remember about such examples seen only a few times during training and how long does that memory persist in the face of continuous training with new examples? Here, we investigate these questions through simple recognition, recall, and retention experiments with LLMs. In recognition experiments, we ask if the model can distinguish the seen example from a novel example; in recall experiments, we ask if the model can correctly recall the seen example when cued by a part of it; and in retention experiments, we periodically probe the model's memory for the original examples as the model is trained continuously with new examples. We find that a single exposure is generally sufficient for a model to achieve near perfect accuracy even in very challenging recognition experiments. We estimate that the recognition performance of even small language models easily exceeds human recognition performance reported in similar experiments with humans (Shepard, 1967). Achieving near perfect recall takes more exposures, but most models can do it in just 3 exposures. The flip side of this remarkable capacity for fast learning is that precise memories are quickly overwritten: recall performance for the original examples drops steeply over the first 10 training updates with new examples, followed by a more gradual decline. Even after 100K updates, however, some of the original examples are still recalled near perfectly. A qualitatively similar retention pattern has been observed in human long-term memory retention studies before (Bahrick, 1984). Finally, recognition is much more robust to interference than recall and memory for natural language sentences is generally superior to memory for stimuli without structure.
abstract_zh: 现代大语言模型 (LLM) 的训练是在这样一种情况下进行的：在训练过程中，模型仅会看到大多数训练示例几次。对于在训练过程中只出现过几次的此类示例，模型会记住什么？在面对新示例的连续训练时，这种记忆会持续多久？在这里，我们通过法学硕士的简单识别、回忆和记忆实验来研究这些问题。在识别实验中，我们询问模型是否能够区分看到的例子和新的例子；在回忆实验中，我们询问模型在收到示例的一部分提示时是否能够正确回忆出所看到的示例；在保留实验中，随着模型不断使用新示例进行训练，我们会定期探测模型对原始示例的记忆。我们发现，即使在非常具有挑战性的识别实验中，单次曝光通常足以让模型达到近乎完美的精度。我们估计，即使是小型语言模型的识别性能也很容易超过人类类似实验中报告的人类识别性能（Shepard，1967）。实现近乎完美的回忆需要更多的曝光，但大多数模型只需 3 次曝光即可做到。这种卓越的快速学习能力的另一面是，精确的记忆很快就会被覆盖：在使用新示例进行的前 10 次训练更新中，原始示例的回忆性能急剧下降，随后又逐渐下降。然而，即使经过 10 万次更新，一些原始示例仍然可以近乎完美地被回忆起来。之前在人类长期记忆保留研究中观察到了性质相似的保留模式（Bahrick，1984）。最后，识别比回忆更能抵抗干扰，并且对自然语言句子的记忆通常优于对无结构刺激的记忆。

AI.tldr: 该论文通过识别、回忆和保留实验，研究大型语言模型（LLMs）在少量训练示例下的记忆能力。
AI.motivation: 现代LLMs的训练中大多数示例仅被模型接触几次，研究其如何记忆这些示例及记忆持续时间对理解模型学习机制至关重要。
AI.method: 设计三种实验：识别（区分已见与新示例）、回忆（通过部分提示回忆完整示例）、保留（持续训练中定期测试原始示例记忆）。
AI.result: 单次接触即可使模型在识别任务中接近完美准确率，远超人类表现；回忆需3次接触，但记忆随新数据快速覆盖，100K次更新后部分示例仍被完美保留。
AI.conclusion: LLMs具有快速学习能力但记忆易被覆盖，其保留模式与人类长期记忆相似，且结构化语言记忆优于无结构刺激。

search_text: Recognition, recall, and retention of few-shot memories in large language models 大型语言模型中少数镜头记忆的识别、回忆和保留 The training of modern large language models (LLMs) takes place in a regime where most training examples are seen only a few times by the model during the course of training. What does a model remember about such examples seen only a few times during training and how long does that memory persist in the face of continuous training with new examples? Here, we investigate these questions through simple recognition, recall, and retention experiments with LLMs. In recognition experiments, we ask if the model can distinguish the seen example from a novel example; in recall experiments, we ask if the model can correctly recall the seen example when cued by a part of it; and in retention experiments, we periodically probe the model's memory for the original examples as the model is trained continuously with new examples. We find that a single exposure is generally sufficient for a model to achieve near perfect accuracy even in very challenging recognition experiments. We estimate that the recognition performance of even small language models easily exceeds human recognition performance reported in similar experiments with humans (Shepard, 1967). Achieving near perfect recall takes more exposures, but most models can do it in just 3 exposures. The flip side of this remarkable capacity for fast learning is that precise memories are quickly overwritten: recall performance for the original examples drops steeply over the first 10 training updates with new examples, followed by a more gradual decline. Even after 100K updates, however, some of the original examples are still recalled near perfectly. A qualitatively similar retention pattern has been observed in human long-term memory retention studies before (Bahrick, 1984). Finally, recognition is much more robust to interference than recall and memory for natural language sentences is generally superior to memory for stimuli without structure. 现代大语言模型 (LLM) 的训练是在这样一种情况下进行的：在训练过程中，模型仅会看到大多数训练示例几次。对于在训练过程中只出现过几次的此类示例，模型会记住什么？在面对新示例的连续训练时，这种记忆会持续多久？在这里，我们通过法学硕士的简单识别、回忆和记忆实验来研究这些问题。在识别实验中，我们询问模型是否能够区分看到的例子和新的例子；在回忆实验中，我们询问模型在收到示例的一部分提示时是否能够正确回忆出所看到的示例；在保留实验中，随着模型不断使用新示例进行训练，我们会定期探测模型对原始示例的记忆。我们发现，即使在非常具有挑战性的识别实验中，单次曝光通常足以让模型达到近乎完美的精度。我们估计，即使是小型语言模型的识别性能也很容易超过人类类似实验中报告的人类识别性能（Shepard，1967）。实现近乎完美的回忆需要更多的曝光，但大多数模型只需 3 次曝光即可做到。这种卓越的快速学习能力的另一面是，精确的记忆很快就会被覆盖：在使用新示例进行的前 10 次训练更新中，原始示例的回忆性能急剧下降，随后又逐渐下降。然而，即使经过 10 万次更新，一些原始示例仍然可以近乎完美地被回忆起来。之前在人类长期记忆保留研究中观察到了性质相似的保留模式（Bahrick，1984）。最后，识别比回忆更能抵抗干扰，并且对自然语言句子的记忆通常优于对无结构刺激的记忆。 该论文通过识别、回忆和保留实验，研究大型语言模型（LLMs）在少量训练示例下的记忆能力。 A. Emin Orhan cs.CL,cs.LG,cs.NE

authors_text: Anaelia Ovalle,Arjun Subramonian,Vagrant Gautam,Gilbert Gee,Kai-Wei Chang
title: Factoring the Matrix of Domination: A Critical Review and Reimagination of Intersectionality in AI Fairness
title_zh: 考虑统治矩阵：人工智能公平中交叉性的批判性回顾和重新想象
abstract: Intersectionality is a critical framework that, through inquiry and praxis, allows us to examine how social inequalities persist through domains of structure and discipline. Given AI fairness' raison d'etre of "fairness", we argue that adopting intersectionality as an analytical framework is pivotal to effectively operationalizing fairness. Through a critical review of how intersectionality is discussed in 30 papers from the AI fairness literature, we deductively and inductively: 1) map how intersectionality tenets operate within the AI fairness paradigm and 2) uncover gaps between the conceptualization and operationalization of intersectionality. We find that researchers overwhelmingly reduce intersectionality to optimizing for fairness metrics over demographic subgroups. They also fail to discuss their social context and when mentioning power, they mostly situate it only within the AI pipeline. We: 3) outline and assess the implications of these gaps for critical inquiry and praxis, and 4) provide actionable recommendations for AI fairness researchers to engage with intersectionality in their work by grounding it in AI epistemology.
summary: Intersectionality is a critical framework that, through inquiry and praxis, allows us to examine how social inequalities persist through domains of structure and discipline. Given AI fairness' raison d'etre of "fairness", we argue that adopting intersectionality as an analytical framework is pivotal to effectively operationalizing fairness. Through a critical review of how intersectionality is discussed in 30 papers from the AI fairness literature, we deductively and inductively: 1) map how intersectionality tenets operate within the AI fairness paradigm and 2) uncover gaps between the conceptualization and operationalization of intersectionality. We find that researchers overwhelmingly reduce intersectionality to optimizing for fairness metrics over demographic subgroups. They also fail to discuss their social context and when mentioning power, they mostly situate it only within the AI pipeline. We: 3) outline and assess the implications of these gaps for critical inquiry and praxis, and 4) provide actionable recommendations for AI fairness researchers to engage with intersectionality in their work by grounding it in AI epistemology.
abstract_zh: 交叉性是一个关键框架，通过探究和实践，我们可以通过结构和学科领域来审视社会不平等如何持续存在。鉴于人工智能公平的“公平”存在理由，我们认为采用交叉性作为分析框架对于有效实施公平至关重要。通过对人工智能公平文献中 30 篇论文如何讨论交叉性进行批判性回顾，我们进行演绎和归纳：1）绘制交叉性原则如何在人工智能公平范式中运作；2）揭示交叉性概念化和操作化之间的差距。我们发现，研究人员极大地减少了交叉性，以优化人口统计亚组的公平性指标。他们也没有讨论他们的社会背景，当提到权力时，他们大多只将其置于人工智能管道中。我们：3）概述并评估这些差距对批判性探究和实践的影响，4）为人工智能公平研究人员提供可行的建议，通过将其扎根于人工智能认识论来参与其工作中的交叉性。

AI.tldr: 批判性回顾并重新构想AI公平性中的交叉性理论，分析其概念与实践的差距
AI.motivation: 现有AI公平性研究未能充分应用交叉性理论，需重新审视其理论与实践的差距
AI.method: 对30篇AI公平性文献进行批判性分析，结合演绎与归纳法，映射交叉性原则的应用并发现概念与操作化的差距
AI.result: 发现研究者过度简化交叉性为子群体公平性优化，忽视社会背景与权力结构分析
AI.conclusion: 呼吁AI公平性研究者基于AI认识论，更深入整合交叉性理论以促进公平性实践
